item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doctor fucking Spanko saves the day by stuffing the ballots

God bless that bird

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Best. SCP. Ever
+1
absolutely perfect, we need more Americans willing to tape bags onto cow asses

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why the hell is Dr. Spanko involved in election conspiracies?
Correct me if I'm wrong, but it seems like he's reporting that the ballots in Florida were successfully stuffed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it's such a small thing in this amazing SCP, but I just wanted to say that I got a good laugh out of this line in particular:

Clinton: Yeah, that's… hmm. That's probably not the reefer, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spironi: What the fuck does any of that mean.

This was the first line of many that actually made me audibly laugh. Very few mainlist articles have ever made me laugh at all, let alone as many times as this one did. And yet, despite how hysterical it was, I was still amazed at how well it fit in typical Foundation universe, as well as how it conveyed the lengths that the Foundation is willing - no, is required - to go, all the way up to rigging national elections and pulling the strings of major global events. I have almost no critiques for this article; I genuinely wish I could upvote more than once.

Also, the debate dialogue was one of the funniest things I've read in months. Absolute fucking gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Bright going on a rant, Al Gore getting an alien jammed in his head, talking dogs, people flying butt-naked wearing only black socks, cows with bags taped to their rears, talking dogs….good God. The only thing this SCP is missing is fucking pipes.

This is the kind of weird shit that makes me enjoy coming here, even if it is a bit on the taboo side of things.

+1, because I can't give more than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a trip from beginning to end. I know you're worried about it being "too lolfoundation" but it's really not. The Foundation manipulating governments and political events is something I expect given how dedicated they are to secrecy, and I love how pissed Jack is and how he manages to maintain that pissitude right to the very end.

+∞

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, was hesitant about the idea, but damn if that wasn't a stellar execution. The dialgoue really killed it for me, and I don't normally like joke SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have fallen in love with this SCP, but there's only one thing bothering me - why would the Foundation make Bright run for a second term?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, to be fair, American foriegn policy often brings about death amd and misfortune in East Asian cultures.

  • awkward rimshot *

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only major gripe that I have about this article is that it occupies the perfect slot for an SCP about the number 4 and its association with death and misfortune in East Asian cultures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yiss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the greatest scp I've ever read. +1
I still can't believe you wrote this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had to reread it, but… I think the implication is that Dr. Spanko is somehow responsible for the Florida election clusterfuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to join the others calling for a President Bright hub. Both because it means more of this, and because we really do need answers to some of the questions asked earlier in this thread. Is Dr. Bright responsible for the war in Iraq, &c.? Why did he run for re-election?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god, this is brilliant.

Up yours kaktus, you get my swear and my plus 1.

item.title: Re: THE ELECTION OF 2000
item.content:encoded: Nah mate I'm good.

item.title: THE ELECTION OF 2000
item.content:encoded: It is too long. Author, I suggest you use collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Towards the beginning of reading this, I was thinking this might be the best thing I've read all month. And I've read a lot of good things this month. However, throughout the wild, WILD ride I am now absolutely sure this is the best thing I've read all year, and my favorite SCP to date. I love the dialogue, the ridiculousness, and everything Garber says and thinks. The ads were fantastic, and if I were American, Jack Bright would be My President. I salute you 500% for running with this idea after you came up with it. Not all men would have as much or the same courage. And boy did it pay off.

Hardest +1 I've ever done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 963 can make mental clones. He's probably Bush 44 and back in an orangutan at Site-19 at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course, the true horror belying this SCP is the fact that Jack Bright got to be president of the USA for eight solid years. My god. It explains so much.

Also raises the question of whether he's still in that body, given that dubs is still visibly alive and publicly active…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly your question initially prompted me to look for some deep dive connection between Bush 44 and the Ticonderoga class that they hid here, but the only thing that I could come up with was that his dad was VP when the Vincennes shot down Flight 655. Which is basically just because all conspiracy theories eventually lead to Bush 41. :)

Bush 44 was in the Air Guard, and Gore was in the Army so not any naval links there.

So I had nothing, but you had left that great straight line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of all possible replies to this comment I had anticipated, I never conceived that I'd be directed to Wikipedia for a lesson in Cold War era naval combat technology. Outstanding.

Will Project Foundation or whatever the hell Staff is calling it allow for upvoting comments?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why the hell was "Ticonderoga" class necessary?

To be a multirole cruiser capable of acting as a flagship and AEGIS platform as the Iowa class and post-war cruisers were being retired.

Folks, I'll be here all night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie i stopped reading before the mass of logs because this leans on tropes that are kinda bullshit imo. Novote, and why the hell was "Ticonderoga" class necessary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I both fought in that war, and helped an antiwar vet run for Congress, so I am cool with chilling out a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's either a novel adverb meaning "hugely" or a garbled pronunciation of "big league" which is a sportsball metaphor meaning "very significant". Nobody seems to know for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Indeedings, Uncle Pointy am are being SEVERAL unwelcomeds, town not bigly sufficient for the bothitude of the me-include.

Out of curiosity, what does "bigly" mean in English?
God bless america.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, but shouldn't Spanko's phone call be in 2001?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a Parisitic Multidimensional Ghost Alien Attached itself to Al Gore in 1998, and then attempted to win the 2000 election.

And I thought the Hentai Goddess was weird

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am meh on this. Some parts did made me laugh but I can't take it seriously as a part of the wider canon of the SCP universe. As others said, it is perfect for a -J, but I don't picture this as a part of my canon.

I don't agree with the whole wacky Dr. Bright or Kain being a dog for those were products of a time where there were low standards. I don't like how unprofessional and nonchalant people sound at times.

At first, I was confused with the Dr. Spanko tag and then realized where it appeared. I'm iffy on the implication that Spanko is whatever 4444 is. Also, I had no clue what was the point of the transcript for it added nothing. I had no clue what it was saying and it is just word salad. Yes, that is the point but it is more incomprehensible than the previous nonsense spoken by 4444.

I don't know, but since it succeeded in making me laugh I decided to just novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think "SCP-4444 casting a dark spell on the audience…" should be changed to "SCP-4444 casting a "dark spell" on the audience… ". Other than that this skip is really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really, why are we even writing in the internet at all, when we could be better spending our lives righting the wrongs of the world instead of parodying it in short-form fiction?

Really makes you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
maybe when we're out of the "perpetual endless war started by the wacky protagonist that is sapping the resources and lives of millions of people, leading to the slow-motion demise of modern democracy, widespread cynicism of millions of people towards politics, leading to a flagrant rise of nihilistic neo-fascism and imperialism that sees a future American president put children in concentration camps separate from their parents, plus millions of dead afghan and Iraqi civilians" phase in our history, this will be fun and loopy. Or maybe not even then

Also:

Status: Rejected - No towns with reasonably convincing poisoned water supplies.

oppo research isn't even a thing anymore, is it? there's literally entire towns and cities built on top of Superfund sites to this day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just saw the title and thought… Hmm. Completely missing the current vote count, managing to miss the caption on the photo (which made that bit even funnier), I went on to read it and thought "well, this is certainly an odd thing that is happening."

Then I reached that first interview and saw all the author avatars. And I thought as I read, "oh, well, that's nice, I have fond memories of reading the shenanigans, but it's always interesting to see people do serious takes on them-"

"That is a talking dog."

"- oh unholy fracking crap it's the lolFoundation/early series 1-versions of them, this is going to be amazing!"

I spent most of the rest of article laughing - and nothing quite so hard as Bright as Bush, that was perfect - and thought, "well, it's gonna get my vote, but i bet most people aren;t going to like it because of that, but…" and then I was pleasantly surprised to see the vote count.

So, thank you djkaktus, I need that laugh today, and coming from the most recent series of the SCP, the unexpectness of it made it all the more welcome.

I second the motion someone said earlier, I kind of need to see a President Bright hub…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr Bright as the US president? No wonder we got into the Iraqi and Afghan wars! This explains so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, we had 001 (special case), and 022 and 333 which were understandably unnoticed. This makes 4444 the inevitable last of its kind (seriously, 10,000 skips?)
I have to say… it totally deserves it.
"vicarious corncrake caliper"
Oh my god.

item.title: Just realized I never commented.
item.content:encoded: I'm honestly a little mad that I really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are definitely moments where it feels too close to a -J and away from the mainlist, but for the most part the balance is maintained well and an interesting story is told. I enjoyed the easter eggs subtly planted throughout as well (the two SCPs that obviously most influenced this are leaned on but not piggybacked on). It stretches credibility a tad, but the story kept me involved enough that it didn't bother me a whole lot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this article even more because of the fact that it melts the wall between -J and main list so well that you can interpret it either way and it will still be stellar. I didn't think it fit the 4000 slot, but it was in my opinion the best of the contest, if not one of the best SCP's I've ever read. Bravissimo, and take this +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the funniest things I've ever read in my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No one wants to be a one term president — even the ones that don't want to be president.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, this is one of the few non-J entries that has made me laugh out loud, very, very well done and I'd have loved to upvote it during the contest.
Although it didn't really fit the SCP-4000, it is undoubtfully funny and fully deserves that second place (I even liked it more than the actual 4000).
Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: QUADS GET

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the funniest part of this article is that you go in expecting the punchline to involve the Foundation being responsible for the mess in Florida, but then you get to that part and find out that, nope, Florida did that all on its own. Bravo, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guess we'll have to stay tuned for that. My theory is that it's like that one other tale. Bright goes mad with power. (otoh, i swear he's got beef with those terrorists, man. after what happened to him.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just have one question: if Bright was so, ah, uninterested in being President, why did he run for re-election in 2004?

Anyway…I enjoyed this article. A lot. Enough to novote despite the fact that it hits my one hard-and-fast* downvote criterion. I won't go into that, because it's a matter of personal taste rather than quality, but the fact that I otherwise would have upvoted when I very rarely do so is high praise. It's a good article. It's also a fun article. I know some people who otherwise liked it said they didn't want it to win because it would set the tone for the rest of the series, but I myself would have been happy to see a thousand of these.

*[insert childish snickering here]

item.title: Nice.
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the support everyone, I'm glad you all generally seemed to like this! It means a lot to get this sort of response from the community, especially in the wake of the 3K contest. Congrats to PPG et all, and here's to the next 1000!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I think either Clef's or ARD/Hippo entries fit better for that designation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for some reason, I was expecting something like scp-4269 from this among the 4000 contest entries. but that does make me wonder what the heck is really gonna take 4269. Probably something like what scp-069 or 420 already is tbh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Futura header will also change - I'm just waiting until after the contest so I don't need to remake it twice (need to change the numbers to 4444 here before too long). That was good advice, by the way. I mocked the Futura header once ages ago just to see what it looked like and thought it was decent, and then kept it across all of the articles I used it in because I was too lazy to change it.

I'll probably (hopefully) eventually go back and rework all of the rest of them, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fonts are much better now! I'm still not crazy about the Futura header, but the Ticonderoga font feels much more appropriate and formal. The stars are a very nice touch. I also like that you are matching it with the FBI's "Office of the Director" header; the consistency between fonts helps drive home the idea that this is very much a joint operation with the U.S. Government.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I love this, and I say that as someone who voted for W twice.

What I adore about this SCP is that it’s like Sonic Mania or the new Doctor Who: a nostalgic reimagining that takes something old and fun but a little hokey and recreates it as those who loved it always saw it. If future articles successfully draw upon this to take us to some truly weird places, we would all be incredibly blessed.

That said, a part of me hopes this has a limited influence only because I know this sort of thing is incredibly hard to pull off so well. This SCP is just a miracle.
Amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just made me think. This deal with Gore all started in late 1998. So how about scp 4998? (otoh, these events generally happened over 1999, so 4999 could be just as appropriate a number for this scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just noticed something while reading The Things Dr Bright Is Not Allowed To Do At The Foundation.

34. Dr. Bright is not allowed to apply SCP-963 to any major political figures. Again.
